channel.title: People and Mathematics · آدم‌ها و ریاضیات
channel.description: A bilingual journal for teaching, thinking, and conversation in mathematics.

item.title: Counting Like a Beginner · موقعیت مشابه
item.dc:creator: Zohreh Pandi
item.description: Zohreh Pandi puts the teacher in the child's shoes: count with the words of a rhyme to feel what a beginner feels, then read a ten-frame without counting. In English for the first time, now interactive. Originally WikiLetter No. 15. — Zohreh Pandi

item.title: Long-term sense making in arithmetic and algebra · مسیر طولانی ملموس‌شدن حساب و جبر
item.dc:creator: David Tall
item.description: David Tall on how a spoken pause carries meaning: slide the gap in 2 + 2 × 2 — and hear each reading aloud — to watch the answer change. First published as WikiLetter No. 5. — David Tall

item.title: The Machine She Didn’t Have · ماشینی که نداشت
item.dc:creator: Behrooz Parhami
item.description: A companion to the lead feature: Behrooz Parhami takes Firoozbakht’s conjecture from what a prime is to the bounds it beats — then reckons what testing it on a 1982 mainframe would truly have cost, and proves brute force was never an option. With three interactive playgrounds. — Behrooz Parhami

item.title: Women's Week in Mathematics · رویدادِ زن در ریاضیات
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Haj Manouchehri & Bamdad Torabi
item.description: How students at Sharif built a two-year celebration of Maryam Mirzakhani — and everything it took to hold it. A report kept for the record, in the organizers' own words. — Farnaz Haj Manouchehri & Bamdad Torabi

item.title: Graphs on Donuts · گراف‌ها روی دونات‌ها
item.dc:creator: Aryan Hemmati
item.description: Euler’s formula counts vertices, edges and regions, and always lands on 2 — until the graph refuses to be drawn flat. Aryan Hemmati follows the count onto donuts and beyond, where the answer belongs to the surface itself. An invitation to topological graph theory, with interactive experiments. — Aryan Hemmati

item.title: From a Plane That Is Not a Plane to a Code That Is a Code · از صفحه‌ای که صفحه نیست تا کدی که کد است
item.dc:creator: Kianoosh Shokri
item.description: The Fano plane has seven points and seven lines — and, hiding inside it, block designs, orthogonal arrays, covering arrays and error-correcting codes. Kianoosh Shokri follows the connections, with interactive explorers along the way. — Kianoosh Shokri

item.title: When Topology Tames Data! · وقتی توپولوژی داده‌ها را رام می‌کند!
item.dc:creator: Mahdis Emami
item.description: A dataset is not just a table — it is a cloud of points with a shape. Mahdis Emami introduces topological data analysis: clusters, loops, and the structures that persist. With a persistent-homology playground to play with. — Mahdis Emami

item.title: Structure Is in Your Gaze · ساختار در نگاه است
item.dc:creator: Amir Asghari
item.description: The way we look at a figure changes its mathematics. Rotate a parallelogram on a circle, cover an angle, read one sentence two ways — small classroom moves, now interactive, where a shift of the gaze does the teaching. — Amir Asghari

item.title: Sheaf · بافه
item.dc:creator: Artan Sheshmani
item.description: One idea — how local pieces are glued into something global — met in a short film. Artan Sheshmani made it in Persian and, himself, in English; watch both and each carries something the other doesn’t. — Artan Sheshmani

item.title: What Shape Student Are You? · چه نوع دانشجویی هستید؟
item.dc:creator: Ian Stewart
item.description: Ian Stewart, in memory of David Tall, on “path-dependent logic” — how a student can follow every step faithfully and still reach a contradiction. — Ian Stewart

item.title: A model for recreating a spinner · مدلی برای بازآفرینی یک چرخنده
item.dc:creator: Zohreh Pandi
item.description: A class rebuilt a probability spinner from a box of beads — and, without being told, uncovered the structure hiding inside the answers. — Zohreh Pandi

item.title: The symbol x for the unknown · علامتِ x به‌جای مجهول
item.dc:creator: Abbas Eqbal Āshtiāni
item.description: Where did the sign x, the word algebra, and the “Arabic” numerals come from? A short history from a 1930 Tehran journal — given here in full. — Abbas Eqbal Āshtiāni

item.title: The Silver Matrix · ماتریسِ نقره‌ای
item.dc:creator: Yahya Tabesh
item.description: How four problems from Tehran travelled to the International Mathematical Olympiad — and how one of them reached the Silver Sea. — Yahya Tabesh

item.title: What is a circle? · دایره چیست؟
item.dc:creator: Amir Asghari
item.description: It enters school in the first grade and returns every year after — yet a student can reach the end of school still unable to say what a circle is. A walk through nine years of textbooks. — Amir Asghari

item.title: Maryam Mirzakhani, in Your Own Hand · مریم میرزاخانی، به دستِ شما
item.dc:creator: A gallery
item.description: A 1946 newspaper clipping set against Maryam Mirzakhani on the cover of the Notices, followed by portraits of her from artists around the world. The section stays open. — A gallery
